top_stories.label: Top Stories
top_stories.ai_summary: The Middle East dominates: Trump says a US–Iran deal to end the war will be signed Sunday, though Tehran is publicly questioning the timing and protesters gathered outside its foreign ministry. World leaders are converging on a tense G7 in Switzerland — where voters are also deciding whether to cap the population at 10 million — amid Geneva protests. Elsewhere, a US air strike killed a Venezuelan gang leader, Anthropic pulled its top AI models on US government orders, and the New York Knicks ended a 50-year title drought.

stories.headline: US and Iran inch closer to deal to end war; Trump says it will be signed Sunday, Tehran questions timing
stories.summary: President Trump said a deal to end the Middle East war would be signed on Sunday, but Iranian officials publicly questioned the timing as negotiators said the two sides were edging closer without a confirmed schedule. Dozens protested against the prospective deal outside Iran's foreign ministry.

stories.headline: Trump to meet Middle East leaders and join Ukraine session at G7 as protests grip Geneva
stories.summary: US officials said Trump will meet Middle East leaders and attend a Ukraine session at the G7 summit in Switzerland. Thousands of demonstrators were expected in Geneva ahead of the gathering.

stories.headline: US air strike kills leader of Venezuela's Tren de Aragua gang, Trump says
stories.summary: Trump said a US–Venezuela operation killed the leader of the Tren de Aragua criminal gang in an air strike. US authorities described the target as a senior figure in the transnational group.

stories.headline: Switzerland votes on proposal to cap population at 10 million
stories.summary: Swiss voters are deciding in a referendum on a proposal to cap the country's population at 10 million, a measure backed by the right that would force curbs on immigration if approved.

stories.headline: Anthropic suspends advanced AI models after US government security order
stories.summary: Anthropic said it would abruptly disable its most advanced models, Claude Fable 5 and Mythos 5, after the US government ordered it to suspend access for foreign nationals over national-security concerns.

stories.headline: Bangladesh PM picks Malaysia and China before India for debut overseas tour
stories.summary: Bangladesh's prime minister chose Malaysia and China ahead of India for his first overseas trip, a sequencing CNA reports signals shifting regional alignments in South Asia.

stories.headline: Trump's name removed from Kennedy Center after court order
stories.summary: Trump's name was removed from Washington's Kennedy Center in a predawn operation after a judge declined to halt the change, capping a legal dispute over the naming.

stories.headline: New York Knicks win first NBA championship in over 50 years
stories.summary: The New York Knicks claimed their first NBA title in more than five decades, ending one of the league's longest championship droughts.

stories.headline: British forces intercept Russian shadow-fleet oil tanker in English Channel
stories.summary: British forces intercepted a Russian "shadow fleet" oil tanker attempting to cross the English Channel, part of Western efforts to pressure the covert vessels used to move sanctioned Russian oil.

singapore.label: Singapore
singapore.ai_summary: Transport rules lead the day: from June 15, Singapore's revised driving-licence requirements extend to all EVs up to 3,000kg, and LTA is warning against devices that keep seat belts slack. The government says it is taking a targeted, national-interest approach to online regulation. On the lighter side, CJ Hendry's Flower Market has been extended to June 15 by popular demand, (G)I-dle packed out their Syncopation concert, and a Tampines blender mishap had everyone wincing. Hot and stormy weather continues, with thundery showers and highs around 34°C.

stories.headline: Revised driving-licence requirements extended to all EVs up to 3,000kg from June 15
stories.summary: From June 15, Singapore's revised driving-licence requirements apply to all electric vehicles weighing up to 3,000kg, extending rules previously focused on heavier EVs as more such models hit the roads.

stories.headline: LTA warns against keeping seat belts slack with clips and other devices
stories.summary: The Land Transport Authority warned motorists against using clips and gadgets that keep seat belts slack, saying the practice undermines the belt's protection and can be an offence.

stories.headline: Singapore takes targeted approach to online regulation, focused on national interest: Sim Ann
stories.summary: Senior Minister of State Sim Ann said Singapore takes a targeted approach to regulating online content, prioritising national interest rather than broad censorship.

stories.headline: Passenger rescued after being trapped in lorry following Woodlands accident
stories.summary: A passenger was rescued after being trapped in a lorry following an accident in Woodlands, with emergency crews extricating the person at the scene.

stories.headline: Pasir Ris-Changi residents to get discounted food and services under new programme
stories.summary: A new community programme will offer Pasir Ris-Changi residents discounts on food and services, aimed at easing cost-of-living pressures in the area.

stories.headline: Singapore study links gut health to fatty liver disease
stories.summary: A Singapore study has found associations between gut health and fatty liver disease, pointing to the microbiome as a potential factor researchers say warrants further investigation.

stories.headline: CJ Hendry's Flower Market extended to June 15 amid 'overwhelming demand'
stories.summary: Artist CJ Hendry's popular Flower Market installation has been extended to June 15 after what organisers called overwhelming demand from visitors.

stories.headline: (G)I-dle bring charisma and powerful vocals — and a JJ Lin cover — to Singapore concert
stories.summary: K-pop group (G)I-dle delivered immense charisma and powerful vocals at their Syncopation concert in Singapore, including a crowd-pleasing cover of JJ Lin, in a review of the show.

stories.headline: Woman allegedly gets hand caught in blender at Tampines condo, taken to hospital
stories.summary: A woman was taken to hospital after allegedly getting her hand caught in a blender at a Tampines condominium, an incident that drew attention online.

wars.label: Wars & Conflicts
wars.ai_summary: The Israel–Iran/US track is the day's pivot: Trump says a deal to end the war will be signed Sunday, but Tehran is questioning the timing and military activity still flickers near the Strait of Hormuz. In Lebanon, Israel kept up strikes on the south and east with broad evacuation warnings. The Russia–Ukraine war grinds on, with a Ukrainian drone strike on a Russian port and a British interception of a Russian shadow-fleet tanker, even as Ukraine is set for a G7 session.

theatres.name: US / Israel–Iran (Strait of Hormuz)
theatres.status: Peace deal said to be imminent but unconfirmed; Iran questions timing; sporadic military activity near the Strait of Hormuz

timeline.event: Trump said a deal to end the Middle East war would be signed on Sunday; Iran questioned the timing as a separate flare-up of military action was reported near the Strait of Hormuz.
US and Iran said to be inching closer to a deal, with the timing still unclear.
Dozens protested against the prospective peace deal outside Iran's foreign ministry, according to local media.

theatres.name: Israel–Lebanon / Gaza
theatres.status: Israeli strikes on southern and eastern Lebanon continue amid broad evacuation warnings

timeline.event: Lebanon reported Israeli strikes on the south as Israel issued broad evacuation warnings.
Lebanon reported strikes on the south and east as Israel widened its evacuation warnings.

theatres.name: Russia–Ukraine
theatres.status: War continues; Ukraine strikes Russian ports while the West targets the shadow fleet; Ukraine set for a G7 session

timeline.event: A Ukrainian drone strike killed one person and sparked a fire at the Russian port of Temryuk, the regional governor said.
British forces intercepted a Russian "shadow fleet" oil tanker attempting to cross the English Channel.
US officials said Trump would attend a Ukraine session at the G7 summit in Switzerland.

outbreaks.label: Outbreaks
outbreaks.ai_summary: The Bundibugyo Ebola epidemic in the Democratic Republic of the Congo and Uganda is escalating sharply: WHO's latest Disease Outbreak News (13 June) reports 695 confirmed cases and 138 deaths, with the rapid rise partly reflecting expanded testing of a backlog of samples. The DRC accounts for the overwhelming majority of cases. The earlier MV Hondius cruise-ship hantavirus cluster has had no new WHO update in over two weeks and appears to have stabilised, so it is not tracked as active this run.

outbreaks.name: Bundibugyo virus (Ebola) — DR Congo & Uganda
outbreaks.status: Escalating; cases rising rapidly in DR Congo, partly due to scaled-up testing
outbreaks.scope_note: WHO DON607 (13 Jun 2026): 695 confirmed cases (676 DR Congo + 19 Uganda) and 138 deaths (incl. 2 in Uganda); at least 37 recovered. DRC figures as of 10 Jun, Uganda as of 11 Jun. Confirmed cases only.

stories.headline: WHO: Bundibugyo Ebola cases surge to 695 with 138 deaths in DR Congo and Uganda
stories.summary: WHO's latest Disease Outbreak News (13 June) reported 695 confirmed Bundibugyo virus disease cases and 138 deaths across the DRC and Uganda — a rapid increase since the 8 June report, driven in part by expanded testing of a backlog of samples. The DRC accounts for 676 cases; Uganda has 19. WHO says at least 37 people have recovered.

cybersecurity.label: CyberSecurity
cybersecurity.ai_summary: Patch priorities lead this cycle: critical unauthenticated RCE flaws in Splunk Enterprise (CVE-2026-20253, CVSS 9.8) and Ivanti Sentry (CVE-2026-10520, CVSS 10.0) headline a busy week, with Ivanti and an Oracle PeopleSoft zero-day (CVE-2026-35273) already exploited and added to CISA's KEV catalog. Supply-chain and AI-agent risks are prominent too — 400+ hijacked Arch Linux AUR packages, a decade-long Chinese auth-flow backdoor, and several AI coding-agent abuse techniques. Big breaches round it out, from Novo Nordisk to a French government messenger and a Japanese energy firm.

stories.headline: Critical Splunk Enterprise flaw allows unauthenticated remote code execution
stories.summary: Splunk released updates for a critical flaw in Splunk Enterprise that allows unauthenticated file operations and remote code execution. Tracked as CVE-2026-20253 (CVSS 9.8), it should be patched promptly.

stories.headline: China-linked hackers backdoored Linux login software to spy on isolated network for nearly a decade
stories.summary: Researchers found China-linked attackers had hijacked the authentication flow of Linux login software, maintaining covert access to an isolated network for nearly ten years before discovery.

stories.headline: Over 400 Arch Linux AUR packages hijacked to deploy infostealer and eBPF rootkit
stories.summary: Attackers took over more than 400 packages in the Arch User Repository (AUR) and rewrote their build scripts to install a Rust-based credential stealer — and an eBPF rootkit when run with root — on any machine that built them.

stories.headline: US orders Anthropic to suspend Fable 5 and Mythos 5 access for foreign nationals
stories.summary: Anthropic said it will disable its most advanced models, Claude Fable 5 and Mythos 5, after the US government ordered access suspended for foreign nationals on national-security grounds — a move with broad implications for AI users worldwide.

stories.headline: ShinyHunters exploit Oracle PeopleSoft zero-day to breach universities
stories.summary: The ShinyHunters extortion crew (tracked by Mandiant as UNC6240) exploited an Oracle PeopleSoft zero-day, CVE-2026-35273 (CVSS 9.8), to steal data, hitting universities hardest. CISA added the flaw to its Known Exploited Vulnerabilities catalog.

stories.headline: CISA orders agencies to patch actively exploited Ivanti Sentry flaw
stories.summary: CISA gave federal agencies three days to patch an actively exploited OS command-injection flaw in Ivanti Sentry, CVE-2026-10520 (CVSS 10.0), which lets an unauthenticated remote attacker gain root-level code execution.

stories.headline: LangGraph flaw chain exposes self-hosted AI agents to remote code execution
stories.summary: Researchers disclosed three now-patched flaws in LangChain's LangGraph framework, including a critical chain that could lead to remote code execution on self-hosted AI agents. Users should update to patched versions.

stories.headline: phpBB forum fixes authentication-bypass bug present for a decade
stories.summary: The phpBB project patched an authentication-bypass vulnerability that had lurked in the popular open-source forum software for about a decade, prompting admins to update their installations.

stories.headline: Pharma giant Novo Nordisk discloses breach of clinical-trials data
stories.summary: Novo Nordisk disclosed a security breach affecting clinical-trials data, the latest major healthcare/pharma organisation to report a data-exposure incident.

stories.headline: French government says Tchap messenger breach affected over 73,000 accounts
stories.summary: France's government said a breach of its Tchap secure-messaging service affected more than 73,000 accounts, exposing user data tied to the state's internal communications platform.

stories.headline: Agentjacking attack tricks AI coding agents into running malicious code
stories.summary: Tenet Security described "Agentjacking," a new class of attack that can trick AI coding agents into running arbitrary code on developer machines, for example via a fake error message that steers the agent.

stories.headline: New GreatXML exploit bypasses Windows BitLocker via recovery-partition XML files
stories.summary: A security researcher released GreatXML, a Windows BitLocker bypass abusing recovery-partition XML files, published a day after a related Microsoft Defender exploit by the same researcher.

stories.headline: INTERPOL takes down Sniper Dz phishing platform, arrests administrator
stories.summary: An INTERPOL-coordinated operation dismantled the Sniper Dz phishing-as-a-service platform and arrested its administrator, disrupting a tool used to run large-scale credential-theft campaigns.

stories.headline: Maine breach-notification portal abused to publish fake disclosures
stories.summary: Maine disabled its data-breach notification portal after attackers abused it to publish fake breach disclosures, an unusual abuse of an official state transparency tool.

stories.headline: Japanese energy firm loses drive holding data of 10.9 million clients
stories.summary: A Japanese energy company reported losing a storage drive containing the data of 10.9 million clients, exposing a large volume of customer information.

stories.headline: The Gentlemen ransomware claims 478 victims and can spread like a worm
stories.summary: Analysis of The Gentlemen ransomware operation found it claims 478 victims and includes worm-like self-spreading capability, having grown from a RaaS affiliate leveraging schemes such as LockBit.

stories.headline: OceanLotus targets Vietnamese investors with SPECTRALVIPER backdoor
stories.summary: The Vietnam-aligned threat actor OceanLotus was tied to two campaigns deploying the SPECTRALVIPER backdoor against domestic entities and stock investors in a prolonged cyber-espionage operation.
